annotations.label: Character of a representation

anchor.snippet: The character of ρ is the function

annotations.note: `FDRep.character V g := LinearMap.trace k V (V.ρ g)` is exactly the character of a representation.

annotations.label: Irreducible character

anchor.snippet: A character χ ρ is called irreducible or simple if ρ is an irreducible representation

annotations.note: Mathlib has `FDRep.character` and a `CategoryTheory.Simple` class on `FDRep`, but no separate `irreducibleCharacter` definition.

annotations.label: Degree of a character

anchor.snippet: The degree of the character χ is the dimension of ρ

annotations.note: `FDRep.char_one : V.character 1 = Module.finrank k V` is the equality of degree of χ with dim ρ.

annotations.label: Linear character

anchor.snippet: A character of degree 1 is called linear

annotations.note: No `linearCharacter` definition for degree-1 group representation characters appears in `Mathlib/RepresentationTheory`.

annotations.label: Kernel of a character

anchor.snippet: the kernel of the character χ ρ is the normal subgroup

annotations.note: Searches for `character.*kernel`/`ker.*character` in `RepresentationTheory` find nothing; the normal subgroup `{g : χ(g) = χ(1)}` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Characters are class functions

anchor.snippet: Characters are class functions

annotations.note: `FDRep.char_conj : V.character (h * g * h⁻¹) = V.character g` is exactly the class-function property.

annotations.label: Irreducible characters form basis of class functions

anchor.snippet: the set of irreducible characters of a given group G into a field F form a basis

annotations.note: No `Basis` of the space of class functions indexed by irreducible representations is present in `Mathlib/RepresentationTheory`.

annotations.label: Isomorphic representations have the same character

anchor.snippet: Isomorphic representations have the same characters

annotations.note: `FDRep.char_iso : (V ≅ W) → V.character = W.character` is precisely this statement.

annotations.label: Character determines representation (char 0)

anchor.snippet: two representations are isomorphic if and only if they have the same character

annotations.note: Related character/norm criteria exist (e.g. `simple_iff_char_is_norm_one`) but the full theorem `χ_V = χ_W ↔ V ≅ W` in char 0 is not formalized.

annotations.label: Character of a direct sum

anchor.snippet: If a representation is the direct sum of subrepresentations

annotations.note: No `char_directSum`/`char_add` lemma in `Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.Character`; only tensor, dual, and linHom are stated.

annotations.label: Restriction of a character to a subgroup

anchor.snippet: If a character of the finite group G is restricted to a subgroup H

annotations.note: Although `Rep.res`/restriction functors exist, no lemma expressing `(Res V).character = V.character ∘ inclusion` is formalized.

annotations.label: Character values are sums of roots of unity

anchor.snippet: Every character value χ ( g ) is a sum of n m -th roots of unity

annotations.note: No lemma relating character values of finite groups to sums of roots of unity is found in `Mathlib.RepresentationTheory`.

annotations.label: Algebraic integrality for irreducible characters

anchor.snippet: If F = C and χ is irreducible, then

annotations.note: The integrality of `|G|·χ(g)/χ(1)` for irreducible χ is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Number of irreducible characters equals number of conjugacy classes

anchor.snippet: the number of irreducible characters of G is equal to the number of conjugacy classes of G

annotations.note: No bijection or cardinality equality between irreducible characters and conjugacy classes is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Degrees of irreducible characters divide |G|

anchor.snippet: the degrees of the irreducible characters are divisors of the order of G

annotations.note: Frobenius's divisibility theorem for irreducible character degrees is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arithmetic identities for characters

anchor.snippet: Let ρ and σ be representations of G . Then the following identities hold

annotations.note: Some identities are formalized (`char_tensor`, `char_dual`, `char_linHom`) but the full list (e.g. symmetric/alternating powers, direct sum) is not.

annotations.label: Character table is square

anchor.snippet: The character table is always square

annotations.note: No `characterTable` object is defined in Mathlib, so its squareness is not stated.

annotations.label: Character table of cyclic group of order 3

anchor.snippet: the cyclic group with three elements and generator u

annotations.note: No explicit computation of the character table of `ZMod 3` (or any specific small group) is present.

annotations.label: Inner product on class functions

anchor.snippet: The space of complex-valued class functions of a finite group G has a natural inner product

annotations.note: The averaging pairing `(1/|G|) Σ χ(g)·ψ(g⁻¹)` appears explicitly in `char_orthonormal` but is not packaged as a named inner product on class functions.

annotations.label: Row orthogonality of irreducible characters

anchor.snippet: the irreducible characters form an orthonormal basis for the space of class-functions

annotations.note: `FDRep.char_orthonormal` formalizes the orthonormality `⟨χ_V, χ_W⟩ = [V≅W]` but the full statement that irreducible characters form a basis of class functions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Column orthogonality

anchor.snippet: applying the same inner product to the columns of the character table yields

annotations.note: The second orthogonality (column) relation for character tables is not formalized.

annotations.label: Order of G from first column of character table

anchor.snippet: The order of G is given by the sum of the squares of the entries of the first column

annotations.note: The identity `Σ (dim V_i)² = |G|` is not stated as a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Centralizer order from column sums

anchor.snippet: the sum of the squares of the absolute values of the entries in any column gives the order of the centralizer

annotations.note: No formalization of `Σ_i |χ_i(g)|² = |C_G(g)|`.

annotations.label: Normal subgroups recognised from character table

anchor.snippet: All normal subgroups of G

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have any character-table-based recognition of normal subgroups.

annotations.label: Kernel of a character from its table values

anchor.snippet: The kernel of a character χ is the set of elements g in G for which χ ( g ) = χ (1)

annotations.note: The kernel-of-character definition (and its equality to `{g : χ(g) = χ(1)}`) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Normal subgroups as intersections of character kernels

anchor.snippet: Each normal subgroup of G is the intersection of the kernels of some of the irreducible characters

annotations.note: No formalization of normal subgroups as intersections of irreducible character kernels.

annotations.label: Commutator subgroup as intersection of linear character kernels

anchor.snippet: The commutator subgroup of G is the intersection of the kernels of the linear characters of G

annotations.note: `commutator G = ⋂ ker(linear χ)` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian iff irreducible characters all linear

anchor.snippet: G is abelian iff each conjugacy class is a singleton

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have the equivalence `IsAbelian G ↔ all irreducible characters are linear`.

annotations.label: Q and D_4 share a character table

anchor.snippet: the quaternion group Q and the dihedral group of 8 elements

annotations.note: No comparison of character tables for `Quaternion` and `DihedralGroup 4` is formalized.

annotations.label: Linear characters form a group (character group)

anchor.snippet: The linear representations of G are themselves a group under the tensor product

annotations.note: No `Group` instance on the set of linear characters / `Hom(G, k^×)` under tensor product appears in `RepresentationTheory`.

annotations.label: Frobenius reciprocity / induced character

anchor.snippet: there is a unique class function θ G of G with the property that

annotations.note: Frobenius reciprocity is formalized at the representation level via the induction-restriction adjunction `Rep.indResAdjunction`, but the character-level statement (induced character via integration against trivial extension) is not.

annotations.label: Explicit formula for the induced character

anchor.snippet: This induced character vanishes on all elements of G which are not conjugate to any element of H

annotations.note: The explicit averaging formula for the induced character `θ^G(g)` is not stated.

annotations.label: Permutation character

anchor.snippet: When θ is the trivial character of H , the induced character obtained is known as the permutation character

annotations.note: No `permutationCharacter` (or `Ind(trivial)`) decl is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mackey's formula

anchor.snippet: Mackey's formula states that

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Mackey decomposition (`loogle "Mackey"` returns zero hits).

annotations.label: Inner product of two induced class functions

anchor.snippet: yields a well-known and useful formula for the inner product of two class functions

annotations.note: The Mackey-style inner product formula for two induced class functions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Character at identity equals dimension

anchor.snippet: its value at the identity is the dimension of the space

annotations.note: `FDRep.char_one : V.character 1 = Module.finrank k V` is exactly χ(1) = dim V.

annotations.label: Character of a Lie group representation

anchor.section: Characters of Lie groups and Lie algebras
anchor.snippet: is defined precisely as for any group as

annotations.note: `FDRep.character` works for any monoid/group and therefore subsumes the Lie group case, but there is no Lie-group-specific definition or continuity statement.

annotations.label: Character of a Lie algebra representation

anchor.section: Characters of Lie groups and Lie algebras
anchor.snippet: we can define the character

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.LieCharacter` denotes 1-dimensional Lie characters `L →ₗ⁅R⁆ R`, not the trace `X ↦ tr(ρ(X))` of a Lie algebra representation; the latter is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compatibility of Lie group and Lie algebra characters

anchor.section: Characters of Lie groups and Lie algebras
anchor.snippet: If we have a Lie group representation and an associated Lie algebra representation

annotations.note: The relation `Θ(exp X) = tr(e^{ρ(X)})` between Lie group and Lie algebra characters is not formalized.

annotations.label: Character on Cartan via weight multiplicities

anchor.section: Characters of Lie groups and Lie algebras
anchor.snippet: can easily be computed in terms of the weight spaces

annotations.note: No formalization of the character of a representation on a Cartan as `Σ dim(V_λ) e^λ`.

annotations.label: Weyl character formula

anchor.section: Characters of Lie groups and Lie algebras
anchor.snippet: can be computed more explicitly by the Weyl character formula

annotations.note: The Weyl character formula is not in Mathlib (`Weyl` hits are about Weyl groups in root systems, not character formulas).
